Explored: Connect NoUpdates:true everywhere; messageToMap contract; agent wait = bash poll; bead notes list design forks

Answered: no stream; core=send --await-reply + tgc await <chat>; returns ALL unread + mark-read; stateless (Telegram read-pointer, no --after); default --timeout 300s (structured timeout, not error); first-batch-then-exit; debounce ~2s default (media-groups + msg series), --debounce 0 to disable

Chosen: mechanism = push via gotgproto dispatcher (NoUpdates:false, handlers.NewMessage) for live + deterministic startup drain of accumulated unread (getHistory min_id=read-pointer). Pure push not trusted to redeliver already-unread (getDifference is pts-relative, not read-relative). Rejected B (polling, the costly workaround).

Fixed in c75a313: bots skip rich_message path (useRichPath predicate), routed to entities path. Live-verified bot default send delivers non-empty text; unit test added; ops suite + vet + gofmt clean.

Context gathered: messageToMap reads only m.Message (messages.go:174), ignores m.RichMessage. RichMessage = recursive PageBlock (33 variants) + RichText (29 variants) AST + attached Photos/Documents. markup pkg has MD->rich parser but no rich->MD renderer. Affects read/context/await.

Strategy chosen: vendor-first, core-complete rich->markdown renderer in internal/markup following Instant-View semantics (per tdlib/tweb official clients). Graceful fallback, never drop content (incl details.summary). Upstream to gotd = optional follow-up bead. No gotd render exists; industry-current problem (Bot API 10.1, June 2026).

Clarified: (1) rich->text field + rich:true flag; (2) media as inline MD lines (no download); (3) scope = RichMessage->MD only, reply_markup+entities = separate follow-up beads; (4) Part=true -> auto-fetch full via messages.getRichMessage{Peer,ID} (present in v0.153.0, returns MessagesMessagesClass). Note: getRichMessage needs network, so projection layer needs conn access (arch impact).

Implemented: conditional grouped_id in messageToMap (internal/ops/messages.go) via m.GetGroupedID(); unit test TestMessageToMapGroupedID (present+equal for album member, absent for single). go build + full go test green.
